message:
fix: Classmethod capture handling and import fixes

- Add inside_classmethod flag to track classmethod context
- Fix captured variable access in classmethods (__init_subclass__)
  - Classmethods can't use __self.__captured_* (no self)
  - Fall back to direct outer scope variable access
- Set inside_classmethod for both direct and inherited methods
- Fix unused capture in enumerate loops
  - Emit _ = &var; suppression for loop captures
- Fix abc and _py_abc module imports
  - Changed abc from "std" to "runtime.Lib.abc"
  - Added _py_abc to import registry
- Add self parameter suppression in signature generation

Note: Mutable outer variables in classmethods still hit Zig's
namespace boundary limitation (cannot access mutable var from struct).

files.patch:
@@ -479,6 +479,10 @@ pub const NativeCodegen = struct {
     // True when inside __new__ method - captured vars accessed via __cls, not __self
     inside_new_method: bool,
 
+    // True when inside a classmethod (e.g., @classmethod or __init_subclass__)
+    // Classmethods don't have self/__self, so captured vars need different access pattern
+    inside_classmethod: bool,
+
     // True when current method has mutable self (*@This() vs *const @This())
     // Used to dereference self when returning from methods that mutate and return self
     method_self_is_mutable: bool,
@@ -782,6 +786,7 @@ pub const NativeCodegen = struct {
             .current_class_captures = null,
             .inside_init_method = false,
             .inside_new_method = false,
+            .inside_classmethod = false,
             .method_self_is_mutable = false,
             .current_method_first_param = null,
             .current_class_parent = null,
